content.problemStatement:
You are given a permutation p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1‚Äč,p2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,pN‚Äč of the integers 111 through NNN. You may perform up to KKK operations of the following type:


Choose three pairwise distinct valid indices i1,i2,i3i_1, i_2, i_3i1‚Äč,i2‚Äč,i3‚Äč. Note that these indices do not have to be chosen in increasing order.
Let's denote the values of pi1p_{i_1}pi1‚Äč‚Äč, pi2p_{i_2}pi2‚Äč‚Äč and pi3p_{i_3}pi3‚Äč‚Äč before this operation by v1v_1v1‚Äč, v2v_2v2‚Äč and v3v_3v3‚Äč respectively.
Change pi2p_{i_2}pi2‚Äč‚Äč to v1v_1v1‚Äč, pi3p_{i_3}pi3‚Äč‚Äč to v2v_2v2‚Äč and pi1p_{i_1}pi1‚Äč‚Äč to v3v_3v3‚Äč. In other words, perform a cyclic right shift on the elements pi1,pi2,pi3p_{i_1}, p_{i_2}, p_{i_3}pi1‚Äč‚Äč,pi2‚Äč‚Äč,pi3‚Äč‚Äč in this order.
For example, the permutation (1,4,3,2,5)(1,4,3,2,5)(1,4,3,2,5) can be changed to the permutation (1,5,3,4,2)(1,5,3,4,2)(1,5,3,4,2) in one operation by choosing i1=2i_1 = 2i1‚Äč=2, i2=4i_2 = 4i2‚Äč=4 and i3=5i_3 = 5i3‚Äč=5.

Your task is to sort the permutation in increasing order. Note that you do not have to minimise the number of performed operations.
constraints: 1‚Č§pi‚Č§N1 \le p_i \le N1‚Č§pi‚Äč‚Č§N for each valid iii
p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1‚Äč,p2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,pN‚Äč are pairwise distinct
the sum of all NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105
pi=N‚ąíi+1p_i = N-i+1pi‚Äč=N‚ąíi+1 for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1‚Äč,p2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,pN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If it is impossible to sort the given permutation using at most KKK operations, print a single line containing the integer ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1.
Otherwise, first, print a line containing a single integer MMM (0‚Č§M‚Č§K0 \le M \le K0‚Č§M‚Č§K) ‚Äē the number of operations you want to perform.
Then, print MMM lines describing these operations. For each iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§M1 \le i \le M1‚Č§i‚Č§M), the iii-th of these lines should contain three space-separated integers aia_iai‚Äč, bib_ibi‚Äč and cic_ici‚Äč (pairwise distinct; 1‚Č§ai,bi,ci‚Č§N1 \le a_i, b_i, c_i \le N1‚Č§ai‚Äč,bi‚Äč,ci‚Äč‚Č§N) ‚Äē the indices on which the iii-th operation is performed.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: We can sort the permutation by performing one cyclic right shift operation on the indices 111, 333 and 444.
